member.name: M:Printer.printModule(MlirIR.MlirModule)
member.summary: Serialize an MlirModule to valid MLIR 20 text. Pure function — no I/O. Caller is responsible for writing to disk. Output order: globals, then external func decls, then func definitions (required by MLIR 20).

member.name: M:Printer.sanitizeMlirName(System.String)
member.summary: Sanitize operator characters in MLIR symbol names (e.g., @List_++ → @List_op_pp). Only affects the part after '@'; leaves names without operator chars unchanged.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler.DecisionTree
member.summary: Decision tree — the output of compilation.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler.Clause
member.summary: Internal clause representation for the algorithm.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler.TestPattern
member.summary: A single test: scrutinee must match a constructor with sub-patterns.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler.CtorTag
member.summary: Constructor tags for our language's runtime representation.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler.Accessor
member.summary: How to access a value: the scrutinee root or a sub-field extracted via GEP.

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.compile(MatchCompiler.Accessor,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{System.Tuple{Ast.Pattern,System.Boolean,System.Int32}})
member.summary: Compile a list of match arms into a decision tree. Each arm is (Ast.Pattern, hasGuard, bodyIndex) where bodyIndex identifies which arm body to execute. The scrutinee is given as a root Accessor.

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.genMatch(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{MatchCompiler.Clause})
member.summary: Main recursive decision tree generator.

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.splitClauses(MatchCompiler.Test,Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{MatchCompiler.Clause})
member.summary: Step 4: Split clauses given a selected test. Returns (argsAccessors, matchClauses, noMatchClauses).

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.branchingHeuristic(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{MatchCompiler.Clause})
member.summary: Step 3: Branching heuristic. From the first clause's tests, select the one that appears in the maximum number of other clauses (by matching on (Scrutinee, CtorTag)).

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.substVarEqs(MatchCompiler.Clause)
member.summary: Step 2: Substitute out variable/wildcard equations. Any test whose pattern is effectively a variable (no constructor) gets pushed into bindings. Since we already handle VarPat/WildcardPat in desugarPattern by producing bindings directly (not tests), this function is a no-op for now. It exists for completeness with the paper.

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.desugarPattern(MatchCompiler.Accessor,Ast.Pattern)
member.summary: Convert an Ast.Pattern applied to a given Accessor into a list of Tests and variable bindings. Recurses into nested patterns.

member.name: M:MatchCompiler.ctorArity(MatchCompiler.CtorTag)
member.summary: Return the number of sub-fields for a given constructor tag.

member.name: T:MatchCompiler
member.summary:
Jules Jacobs pattern matching compilation algorithm. Compiles pattern match expressions into binary decision trees that test one constructor at a time: match# a with | C(a1,...,an) => [A] | _ => [B]
Key ideas from the paper: 1. Clause representation: {a1 is pat1, a2 is pat2, ...} => body 2. Variable elimination: push variable/wildcard bindings into the body as let x = a 3. Branching heuristic: pick the test from the first clause present in the max number of clauses 4. Splitting: for selected test a is C(P1,...,Pn): - Case (a): clause has a is C(Q1,...,Qn) → expand sub-patterns → add to [A] - Case (b): clause has a is D(...) where D≠C → add to [B] - Case (c): clause has no test for a → add to both [A] and [B] 5. Base cases: empty clauses → Fail; first clause has no tests → Leaf 6. Recurse on [A] and [B]

member.name: T:Elaboration.FmtSpec
member.summary: Phase 39: Format specifier type for compile-time dispatch.

member.name: M:Elaboration.appendReturnIfNeeded(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpList{MlirIR.MlirOp},MlirIR.MlirValue)
member.summary: Append ReturnOp to a block body only if the block does not already end with a terminator (llvm.unreachable). This allows Raise at the end of a function without generating a dead `return` after `llvm.unreachable`.

member.name: M:Elaboration.testPattern(Elaboration.ElabEnv,MlirIR.MlirValue,Ast.Pattern)
member.summary: Phase 11: Test a pattern against a scrutinee value. Returns (condOpt, testOps, bodySetupOps, bindEnv) where: condOpt = None -> unconditional match (WildcardPat / VarPat) condOpt = Some v -> I1 condition value; test ops must be emitted before the cond_br testOps = ops that compute the condition (run in the test/entry block) bodySetupOps = ops that run at the START of the body block (e.g. ConsPat head/tail loads) bindEnv = env with any variable bindings from the pattern added

member.name: M:Elaboration.bodyReturnsBool(Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 43: Detect whether a function body's final expression returns bool. Traverses through let bindings to find the tail expression.

member.name: M:Elaboration.isBoolExpr(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String},Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Elaboration.FuncSignature},Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 43: Detect whether an expression statically produces a boolean value. Used to populate BoolVars for correct to_string dispatch.

member.name: M:Elaboration.isPtrParamBody(System.String,Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 43-fix: Detect whether a param needs Ptr type (list OR record OR string access in body). Used to correctly type function parameters in the single-arg Lambda case.

member.name: M:Elaboration.isStringReturningExpr(Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Detect whether a function-call expression likely returns a string (Ptr). Used to accumulate known-string variables from let-bindings.

member.name: M:Elaboration.isListParamBody(System.String,Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Detect whether a LetRec/Lambda body uses list patterns on the parameter, indicating the parameter should be typed Ptr (list pointer) rather than I64.

member.name: M:Elaboration.|StripAnnot|(Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 43: Active pattern for stripAnnot — use in match expressions to see through annotations.

member.name: M:Elaboration.stripAnnot(Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 43: Strip Annot/LambdaAnnot wrappers from the outermost position of an expression. Converts LambdaAnnot to Lambda, removing type annotations. Used for pattern matching that needs to see through type annotation wrappers.

member.name: M:Elaboration.coerceToI64Arg(Elaboration.ElabEnv,MlirIR.MlirValue)
member.summary: Phase 39: Coerce a value to I64 for int-typed sprintf wrapper args. I1 → zext to I64; I64 → pass through; Ptr → ptrtoint; I32 → pass through.

member.name: M:Elaboration.fmtSpecTypes(System.String)
member.summary: Phase 39: Return ordered list of format specifier types in a format string. %d, %x, %02x, %c, %ld, %lx → IntSpec; %s → StrSpec; %% → ignored.

member.name: M:Elaboration.coerceToPtrArg(Elaboration.ElabEnv,MlirIR.MlirValue)
member.summary: Phase 35: Coerce a value to Ptr for builtin functions that expect pointer arguments. I64 → inttoptr; Ptr → no-op.

member.name: M:Elaboration.coerceToI64(Elaboration.ElabEnv,MlirIR.MlirValue)
member.summary: Phase 35: Coerce a value to I64 for uniform closure return type. I1 → zext to I64; Ptr → ptrtoint to I64; I64 → no-op.

member.name: M:Elaboration.detectCollectionKind(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpMap{System.String,Elaboration.CollectionKind},Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 34-03: Detect which Phase 33 collection kind an expression produces. Returns Some kind if the expression is a known collection-creating call or variable.

member.name: M:Elaboration.isArrayExpr(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{System.String},Ast.Expr)
member.summary: Phase 30: Determine if an expression is statically known to produce an array (not a list). Used by ForInExpr to select lang_for_in_array vs lang_for_in_list at compile time. Conservative: returns false (assume list) for variables or unknown expressions.

member.name: M:Elaboration.failWithSpan``2(Ast.Span,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.PrintfFormat{``0,Microsoft.FSharp.Core.Unit,System.String,``1})
member.summary: Phase 44: Raise an error with source location in "file:line:col: message" format. Uses Printf.ksprintf to support format strings like failwithf.

member.name: M:Pipeline.compile(MlirIR.MlirModule,System.String)
member.summary: Compile an MlirModule to a native ELF binary at outputPath. Uses temp files for intermediate stages; cleans them up on success or failure.

member.name: M:Pipeline.runTool(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Run an external tool and wait for completion. Returns Ok () on exit 0, Error (exitCode, stderr) otherwise.

member.name: M:Pipeline.resolveTool(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve an LLVM tool binary by name. Search order: 1. $LLVM_BIN_DIR/<name> (explicit override) 2. /opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/<name> (macOS Homebrew) 3. /usr/local/bin/<name> (Linux / manual install)
